feed.title: Patient Comet
feed.subtitle: Independent Intelligence Review — one argument a week on technology, creativity, and AI. Researched to the root, openly opinionated, by Nadim A. Massih.

entry.title: The AI Team Member: Enthusiastic, Error-Prone, and Off Every Invoice
entry.summary: Seven of fourteen agencies surveyed by Digiday in March 2026 were absorbing AI compute costs rather than billing them. AI now works on every project, and almost no one charges for it.

entry.title: AI Anatomy: The Body Most Companies Are Missing
entry.summary: Most companies built a brain and called it a strategy. AI Anatomy maps every part of the body they are missing — memory, hands, a nervous system — and what it takes to build one.

entry.title: The Taste Problem
entry.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri declared in January 2026 that authenticity is becoming infinitely reproducible. When AI can fake polish and effort, the new proof of human presence is specificity, voice, and the visible mark of a real person's perspective.

entry.title: When Code Is Cheap, What Do You Ship?
entry.summary: Snap fired a thousand people because AI writes 65% of its code. The hardest problems in software — requirements, production debugging, good decisions, communication, reliability — have not changed.

entry.title: The Second Customer
entry.summary: AI-sourced traffic to US retail grew 393% in Q1 2026 and now converts 42% better than human traffic. Your product already serves a second user — the AI agent acting on a buyer's behalf.

entry.title: Anyone Can Make It Now
entry.summary: Google made its film studio free. WPP cut a third of its creative headcount. AI handed every team a film studio and recording booth. What separates the winners is not access to the tools.

entry.title: The Last Human Reader
entry.summary: Condé Nast's CEO confirmed AI-driven traffic referrals to Vogue dropped more than 50% in 2025. The primary reader of most published content is no longer human.

entry.title: Own Your AI
entry.summary: DeepSeek trained a world-class model for $294,000 and released it free. AI is shifting from a service you subscribe to, to a feature you ship.

entry.title: LLMflation: The Price of the Wrong Hands
entry.summary: Microsoft cancelled its Claude Code licences after engineers burned through the company's entire annual AI budget in weeks. LLMflation is what happens when AI access outpaces AI governance.

entry.title: The Vibe Coder Fallacy
entry.summary: Three AI-built apps breached in their first 90 days. The gap between a working demo and production-safe software is not closing — it is being hidden by tools that make broken things look finished.
